verses.1: And it came about, when David lived in his house, that David said to Nathan the prophet, “Look, I am living in a house of cedar, but the ark of the covenant of the LORD is under tent curtains.”
verses.2: Then Nathan said to David, “Do whatever is in your heart, for God is with you.”
verses.3: But it happened that same night, that the word of God came to Nathan, saying,
verses.4: “Go and tell David My servant, ‘This is what the LORD says: “You shall not build a house for Me to dwell in;
verses.5: for I have not dwelt in a house since the day that I brought up Israel to this day, but I have gone from tent to tent and from one dwelling place to another.
verses.6: In all places where I have walked with all Israel, have I spoken a word with any of the judges of Israel, whom I commanded to shepherd My people, saying, ‘Why have you not built Me a house of cedar?’”’
verses.7: Now, therefore, this is what you shall say to My servant David: ‘This is what the LORD of armies says: “I took you from the pasture, from following the sheep, to be leader over My people Israel.
verses.8: I have been with you wherever you have gone, and have eliminated all your enemies from you; and I will make for you a name like the name of the great ones who are on the earth.
verses.9: And I will appoint a place for My people Israel, and will plant them there, so that they may live in their own place and not tremble with anxiety again; and the wicked will not make them waste away anymore as they did previously,
verses.10: even from the day that I commanded judges to be over My people Israel. And I will subdue all your enemies. Moreover, I tell you that the LORD will build a house for you.
verses.11: When your days are fulfilled that you must go to be with your fathers, then I will set up one of your descendants after you, who will be from your sons; and I will establish his kingdom.
verses.12: He shall build for Me a house, and I will establish his throne forever.
verses.13: I will be his father and he shall be My son; and I will not take My favor away from him, as I took it from him who was before you.
verses.14: But I will settle him in My house and in My kingdom forever, and his throne will be established forever.”’”
verses.15: According to all these words and according to all of this vision, so Nathan spoke to David.
verses.16: Then King David came in and sat before the LORD, and said, “Who am I, LORD God, and what is my house that You have brought me this far?
verses.17: This was a small thing in Your eyes, God; but You have spoken of Your servant’s house for a great while to come, and have viewed me according to the standard of a person of high degree, LORD God.
verses.18: What more can David still say to You concerning the honor bestowed on Your servant? For You know Your servant.
verses.19: LORD, for Your servant’s sake, and according to Your own heart, You have accomplished all this greatness, to make known all these great things.
verses.20: LORD, there is none like You, nor is there any God besides You, according to everything that we have heard with our ears.
verses.21: And what one nation on the earth is like Your people Israel, whom God went to redeem for Himself as a people, to make for You a name by great and awesome things, by driving out nations from before Your people, whom You redeemed from Egypt?
verses.22: For You have made Your people Israel Your own people forever, and You, LORD, became their God.
verses.23: “Now, LORD, let the word that You have spoken concerning Your servant and concerning his house be established forever, and do just as You have spoken.
verses.24: Let Your name be established and be great forever, saying, ‘The LORD of armies is the God of Israel, a God to Israel; and the house of Your servant David is established before You.’
verses.25: For You, my God, have revealed to Your servant that You will build him a house; therefore Your servant has found courage to pray before You.
verses.26: Now, LORD, You are God, and have promised this good thing to Your servant.
verses.27: And now You have decided to bless the house of Your servant, that it may continue forever before You; for You, LORD, have blessed, and it is blessed forever.”
